home.seoTitle: Skelion Enterprises | Cybersecurity Services, Pentesting, GRC & Licensing
home.seoDesc: Skelion Enterprises delivers offensive security, GRC & ISO certification support, CISO-as-a-Service, cybersecurity training, software licensing and physical security across Africa and beyond.
home.lead: Skelion Enterprises is a full-spectrum cybersecurity partner: offensive security engagements, GRC & ISO certification support, executive security leadership, training, licensed software and physical security, under one roof.
home.assessTitle: Score your security posture in five minutes
home.assessDesc: Fifteen questions across governance, access, infrastructure, people and resilience. Instant graded report with the priorities an auditor would flag first.

services.sub: From breaking into your systems (with permission) to certifying that they meet international standards, Skelion covers the full security lifecycle.

cards.title: Pentesting & Red Teaming
cards.desc: Adversary-grade offensive engagements across internal & external networks, web, API, cloud and AI systems. Findings your board can act on, not just a scanner export.

cards.title: Security Auditing, GRC & ISO Certification Support
cards.desc: Gap assessments, risk management, control design and end-to-end support toward ISO certification. Governance that regulators and clients trust.

cards.title: CISO-as-a-Service
cards.desc: Executive security leadership on demand: strategy, budgets, board reporting, incident oversight and compliance direction, without the full-time cost.

cards.title: Cybersecurity Training
cards.desc: From awareness programs for all staff to hands-on offensive and defensive tracks for technical teams. Delivered in English and French, on-site or remote.

cards.title: Software License Reselling
cards.desc: Genuine licenses from Microsoft, Kaspersky, Fortinet, Adobe, McAfee, VMware, Norton and more, with sizing advice, volume pricing and renewal management.

cards.title: Physical Security Devices
cards.desc: Access control, video surveillance, intrusion detection and biometric systems, specified, supplied and integrated with your digital security stack.

offense.sub: We attack the way real adversaries do, then hand you a prioritized, evidence-backed path to fixing what matters. Every engagement follows a strict rules-of-engagement and ends with retesting.

scopes.title: External Network
scopes.desc: Internet-facing perimeter: exposed services, misconfigurations, credential attacks and exploitation paths from the outside in.

scopes.title: Internal Network
scopes.desc: Assumed-breach testing: lateral movement, privilege escalation, Active Directory attack paths and segmentation validation.

scopes.title: Web Applications
scopes.desc: Deep manual testing of business logic, authentication, access control and injection classes, beyond automated scanning.

scopes.title: API Security
scopes.desc: REST, GraphQL and SOAP testing: broken object-level authorization, mass assignment, rate-limit abuse and data exposure.

scopes.title: Cloud Security
scopes.desc: Configuration review and attack simulation across cloud tenants: identity, storage exposure, privilege paths and workload security.

scopes.title: AI & LLM Systems
scopes.desc: Prompt injection, data leakage, model abuse and integration flaws in AI-powered applications, an attack surface most firms don't test yet.

scopes.title: Full Red Team
scopes.desc: Objective-driven, multi-vector campaigns combining technical intrusion and social engineering to test detection and response end to end.

offense.panelIntro: Offensive work is only useful if it changes decisions. Every Skelion engagement closes with:
deliverables: Executive summary written for boards and regulators
Attack-path narratives showing how findings chain
Debrief workshop with your technical team

licenses.sub: Skelion resells and provisions genuine licenses for leading security and productivity vendors, with honest sizing advice so you buy what you actually need, plus renewal tracking so nothing lapses.
licenses.note: Volume licensing, education and government pricing, migration support and multi-year renewal management available.

grc.sub: Compliance is not paperwork; it's proof. We audit your security posture against recognized frameworks and walk beside you from gap assessment to certification day.
grc.trackADesc: Security governance design, risk assessments, policy frameworks, third-party risk, control testing and internal audit support, mapped to the standards your regulators and clients recognize.
grc.trackBDesc: End-to-end support toward ISO certification: initial gap analysis, ISMS/BCMS design and documentation, control implementation, internal audits, management review preparation and side-by-side support during the certification audit itself.

ciso.bandDesc: Most organizations need CISO-level judgment long before they can justify a CISO-level salary. Skelion places a seasoned security executive inside your organization, fractional, retained, or interim, to own strategy, risk and accountability.

training.sub: Humans are your largest attack surface and your best sensor network. We train both ends of the spectrum, from every employee to your most technical defenders.

courses.title: Certification Preparation
courses.desc: Structured preparation tracks for internationally recognized security certifications, with labs and mentoring.

physical.sub: A firewall can't stop someone walking through an unlocked door. We supply and integrate physical security systems that close the loop between your digital and physical perimeters.

footer.blurb: Full-spectrum cybersecurity: offense, governance, leadership, training, licensing and physical security.

pentesting.seoDesc: Adversary-grade penetration testing and red team engagements: external, internal, web, API, cloud, AI/LLM and full red team, with evidence-backed reporting and retesting.
pentesting.sub: A vulnerability scan tells you what a robot found. A Skelion engagement tells you what a motivated adversary would actually do, and exactly how to stop them. Senior operators, strict rules of engagement, findings that survive board scrutiny.

method.title: Scoping & Rules of Engagement
method.desc: We define targets, objectives, testing windows, escalation contacts and legal authorization before a single packet is sent. No surprises, ever.

method.title: Reconnaissance & Mapping
method.desc: We build the same picture an attacker would: exposed assets, technology fingerprints, identity surfaces and business context that shapes the attack plan.

method.title: Controlled Exploitation
method.desc: Vulnerabilities are validated by safe, controlled exploitation, proving real impact while protecting production stability and your data.

method.title: Post-Exploitation & Chaining
method.desc: We follow the paths that matter: privilege escalation, lateral movement and data-access scenarios that show how findings combine into business risk.

method.title: Reporting & Debrief
method.desc: Executive summary for leadership, technical detail with evidence for engineers, and a live debrief workshop so nothing is lost in translation.

method.title: Remediation Retest
method.desc: Critical findings are retested after your fixes at no extra cost. The engagement ends when the risk is closed, not when the report is sent.

scopes.title: External Network
scopes.desc: Your internet-facing perimeter, tested from the outside in.

scopes.title: Internal Network
scopes.desc: Assumed-breach: what happens after one workstation falls.

scopes.title: Web Applications
scopes.desc: Deep manual testing beyond what scanners can see.

scopes.title: API Security
scopes.desc: REST, GraphQL and SOAP interfaces under adversarial pressure.

scopes.title: Cloud Security
scopes.desc: Configuration review and attack simulation in your tenants.

scopes.title: AI & LLM Systems
scopes.desc: The attack surface most firms have not tested yet.

scopes.title: Full Red Team
scopes.desc: Objective-driven campaigns against detection and response.

roe: Written authorization and defined scope before testing begins
Agreed testing windows and change-freeze awareness
Safe exploitation practices that protect production and data
A live escalation channel to your team throughout the engagement
Confidential handling and secure destruction of engagement data
Evidence for every finding, no unverifiable claims
pentesting.ctaTitle: Ready to see what an adversary sees?
pentesting.ctaDesc: Tell us what you need tested and we respond with a scoped proposal, target list, methodology, timeline and a fixed price.
pentesting.customDesc: Hybrid scopes, sector-specific scenarios, or an environment that does not fit a neat category, describe it and we will scope it honestly.

grc.seoTitle: Security Auditing, GRC & ISO Certification Support | Skelion Enterprises
grc.seoDesc: Gap assessments, risk management, ISO 27001 and ISO 22301 certification support, NIST CSF and COBIT alignment, from first assessment to certification day.
grc.sub: Frameworks don't protect organizations; disciplined implementation does. We audit honestly, design controls that fit your reality, and stay beside you until the certificate is on the wall.
grc.trackADesc: For organizations that need to understand and improve their posture: governance design, risk assessment, policy frameworks, third-party risk and control testing mapped to the standards your regulators recognize.
grc.trackBDesc: For organizations committed to certification: we run the full journey from gap analysis to audit-day support, building a management system your team can actually operate after we leave.
trackBPoints: Side-by-side support during the certification audit

phases.title: Assess
phases.desc: Gap analysis against your target standard, scoped to your business context and risk profile. You get a clear picture of distance-to-certification and a prioritized roadmap.

phases.title: Design
phases.desc: Policies, controls, risk methodology and the management system itself, designed around how your organization actually works, not a copy-paste template pack.

phases.title: Implement
phases.desc: Hands-on deployment with your team: control rollout, evidence collection routines, awareness enablement and the operational habits auditors look for.

phases.title: Certify
phases.desc: Internal audit, management review, corrective actions and calm, experienced support in the room (or on the call) during the external certification audit.

grc.frameworksNote: Also: regional banking regulations and sector-specific requirements, tell us your regulatory context and we map to it.
grc.ctaDesc: Start with a gap analysis. You get an honest read on your posture and a realistic path to certification, timeline, effort and priorities.

ciso.seoDesc: Fractional, retained or interim security leadership: strategy, board reporting, budgets, incident oversight and compliance direction without the full-time executive cost.
ciso.sub: Tools don't set strategy. Frameworks don't argue for budget. Someone has to own security at the executive table, and most organizations need that judgment long before they can justify the payroll. That's the gap we fill.

models.title: Fractional CISO
models.desc: A defined share of a senior security executive's week, every week. Continuous ownership of strategy, risk and governance, sized to your organization's stage.
models.fit: Fits: growing organizations that need standing leadership without a full-time seat.

models.title: Retained Advisory
models.desc: Executive counsel on call: board preparation, major decisions, vendor selection, incident oversight. Leadership depth exactly when the stakes are high.
models.fit: Fits: organizations with capable teams that need senior judgment at key moments.

models.title: Interim CISO
models.desc: Full-intensity leadership through a transition: a departure, an incident aftermath, a certification push or the run-up to hiring your first permanent CISO.
models.fit: Fits: organizations in transition that can't afford a leadership vacuum.

first.title: Understand
first.desc: Posture review, risk conversation with leadership, quick-win identification and a clear-eyed read of where the organization actually stands.

first.title: Prioritize
first.desc: Risk register, security roadmap and budget picture, sequenced by business impact, not by vendor marketing.

first.title: Operate
first.desc: Governance rhythm in place: reporting cadence, decision forums, metrics that leadership understands and the roadmap in motion.

ciso.ctaTitle: Put a CISO at your table
ciso.ctaDesc: Tell us about your organization and the pressure you're under. We propose the model, cadence and focus that fits, honestly, including whether you need us at all.

training.seoDesc: Security awareness, offensive and defensive skills, secure development, executive briefings and certification preparation, delivered in English and French, on-site or remote.
training.sub: Slides don't stop phishing. Practice does. Every Skelion track is built around doing, simulations, labs, exercises and decisions, because that's what people remember under pressure.

tracks.title: Security Awareness Programs
tracks.desc: Phishing simulation campaigns, social engineering resilience and secure daily habits. Designed for measurable behavior change, with metrics you can put in front of leadership.

tracks.title: Offensive Security Skills
tracks.desc: Hands-on penetration testing fundamentals: web and network attack techniques taught in labs, by people who run real engagements.

tracks.title: Defensive Operations
tracks.desc: Detection engineering, incident response and hardening workshops for the teams who defend production, built around realistic attack scenarios.

tracks.title: Secure Development
tracks.desc: Secure coding practices, OWASP-aligned threat awareness and code review discipline that fits into how your developers already work.

tracks.title: Board & Leadership Briefings
tracks.desc: Cyber risk literacy for the people who sign off on it: obligations, crisis decision-making and the questions that separate governance from theater.

tracks.title: Certification Preparation
tracks.desc: Structured preparation tracks for internationally recognized security certifications, with labs, mentoring and exam strategy.

delivery: English and French, same quality in both languages
On-site at your facilities or fully remote
Cohort programs, intensive workshops or recurring sessions
Content adapted to your sector and real environment
Hands-on labs and simulations, not passive slideware
Post-training measurement so you can prove the impact
training.ctaDesc: Tell us who needs training and what outcome you're after. We design the track, the format and the measurement around your team.

licenses.seoDesc: Genuine licenses from Microsoft, Fortinet, Kaspersky, VMware, Adobe, McAfee, Norton and more, with honest sizing advice, volume pricing and renewal management.
licenses.sub: Counterfeit and grey-market licenses are a security risk wearing a discount. We provision genuine licenses from authorized channels, and we'll tell you when the smaller SKU is enough, because that's what a security partner does.

process.title: Needs Assessment
process.desc: We look at what you're protecting, what you already own and what you actually use, before recommending anything.

process.title: Honest Sizing
process.desc: Right-sized recommendations: editions, seat counts and terms matched to your real requirements, with volume, education and government pricing where you qualify.

process.title: Provisioning
process.desc: Genuine licenses from authorized channels, delivered with activation support and migration help where you're switching platforms.

process.title: Renewal Management
process.desc: Multi-year tracking so nothing lapses silently: renewal alerts, true-up reviews and re-sizing when your organization changes.

licenses.whyTitle: Why buy through a security firm
why: Genuine licenses, authorized channels, verifiable provenance
Sizing advice from people who deploy these tools in anger
Security-first bundling: licensing decisions tied to your actual risk
Volume, education and government pricing where applicable
Renewal tracking and true-up management across vendors
One accountable partner for the license and the security around it
licenses.ctaDesc: Tell us your vendor, headcount and current setup. We come back with honest sizing and clear pricing, including when the answer is 'you don't need to buy anything'.

physical.seoDesc: Access control, video surveillance, intrusion detection and biometric systems, specified, supplied and integrated with your digital security stack.
physical.sub: Your firewall is world-class and your server-room door is held open with a chair. Physical and digital security fail together, so we design them together.

cats.title: Access Control
cats.desc: Who gets in, where, and when, with an audit trail.

cats.title: Video Surveillance
cats.desc: Coverage designed for evidence, not just deterrence.

cats.title: Intrusion Detection
cats.desc: Know the moment a perimeter is tested.

process.title: Site Survey
process.desc: We walk your facility the way an intruder would: entry points, blind spots, high-value zones and the gaps between policy and practice.

process.title: System Design
process.desc: Device selection and placement engineered for your site and threat profile, integrated with your digital security stack from day one.

process.title: Supply & Installation
process.desc: Genuine equipment, professional installation and configuration hardening, default credentials don't survive our handover.

process.title: Integration & Support
process.desc: Unified visibility with your existing security operations, staff handover training and ongoing support.

convergence: Physical designs informed by our offensive security experience
Devices hardened and network-segmented, cameras are computers too
Access events correlated with your digital security monitoring
Single accountable partner across cyber and physical
Staff training on both digital and physical procedures
Scales from a single office to multi-site deployments

about.seoDesc: Skelion Enterprises is a cybersecurity firm uniting offensive security, governance, and advisory, in English and French.
about.sub: A cybersecurity firm built on a simple conviction: security that has not been tested by a capable adversary is only a hypothesis.
about.missionA: Skelion Enterprises is a cybersecurity firm built on a simple conviction: security that has not been tested by a capable adversary is only a hypothesis.
about.missionB: We bring offensive security, governance and compliance, and executive advisory into a single accountable practice, delivered bilingually across English and French, so organisations can face a hostile threat landscape with evidence rather than assumptions.
approach: Threat-led assessment scoped to your real attack surface
Findings mapped to recognised frameworks and business impact
Senior operators on every engagement, no junior hand-off
Clear remediation guidance, not just a list of problems
Retest and validation to confirm risk is actually closed

values.title: Adversarial mindset
values.desc: We assess systems the way a real attacker would, not the way a checklist assumes.

values.title: Evidence over assertion
values.desc: Every finding is demonstrable and reproducible. We show, we do not merely claim.

values.title: Confidentiality first
values.desc: Engagement data is handled with strict need-to-know discipline and secure custody.

values.title: Business-aligned
values.desc: Security decisions are framed in terms of risk, cost, and operational reality, not fear.

values.title: Bilingual by design
values.desc: We operate fluently in English and French, from reports to briefings to delivery.

values.title: Full-spectrum
values.desc: Offensive testing, governance, and advisory under one accountable team.

about.ctaTitle: Let's talk about your security posture
about.ctaDesc: Whether you are preparing for an audit, an offensive assessment, or a compliance roadmap, we can help.

faq.seoDesc: Answers to common questions on engagements, confidentiality, frameworks, and bilingual delivery at Skelion Enterprises.
faq.sub: The questions we are asked most often about our engagements, our method, and how we work.

items.q: What does a typical engagement look like?
items.a: It begins with scoping to define objectives and boundaries, moves through assessment or advisory work led by senior operators, and ends with a clear report and a remediation path. Where relevant, we retest to confirm issues are closed.

items.q: How do you protect the confidentiality of our data?
items.a: Engagement material is handled on a strict need-to-know basis with secure custody and disciplined data handling throughout. We can align to your own data-handling and residency requirements.

items.q: Which frameworks and standards do you work with?
items.a: We map our work to widely recognised frameworks such as ISO 27001, NIST, and related governance models, and we tailor the reference set to your sector and regulatory context.

items.q: Do you work in both English and French?
items.a: Yes. Reports, briefings, and delivery are available fluently in both English and French, which matters for organisations operating across bilingual and regional environments.
Pricing depends on scope, depth, and duration. We define it transparently during scoping, with no surprises, and we align it to the outcome you need rather than a fixed template.

items.q: Can you support both offensive testing and compliance?
items.a: Yes. Offensive security, governance and compliance, and advisory sit within one team, so testing insight feeds governance and the reverse, without the gaps that come from separate vendors.
Reach out through the contact page or by phone. We will arrange a scoping conversation to understand your objectives and constraints, then propose an approach.

faq.ctaDesc: If your question is not covered here, reach out directly and we will get back to you.

assessment.seoTitle: Free Security Posture Assessment | Skelion Enterprises
assessment.seoDesc: Score your organization's security posture in 5 minutes: governance, access control, infrastructure, people and resilience. Instant graded report with priority recommendations.
assessment.sub: Fifteen questions, five domains, one honest score. Built on the same lens our auditors apply in the field.
assessment.introP1: This assessment measures your security posture across the five domains where breaches are actually won and lost. Each question has three answers: pick the one closest to your reality, not your ambition.
assessment.introP2: It takes about five minutes. You get an instant graded report, domain-by-domain scoring, and priority recommendations. No login required.

gov1.text: Does your organization have documented, management-approved security policies?
gov1.a2: Yes, reviewed at least annually and known by staff
gov1.a1: Some policies exist but are outdated or partial

gov2.text: Is someone formally accountable for information security?
gov2.a2: A dedicated role (CISO or equivalent) with executive access
gov2.a1: Responsibility is shared informally, usually with IT

gov3.text: Do you assess vendors and third parties for security risk?
gov3.a2: A formal process before onboarding and periodically after

iam1.a2: On all critical systems and remote access, without exceptions

iam2.text: Are access rights reviewed and revoked when roles change or people leave?
iam2.a2: A scheduled or automated review process exists
iam2.a1: It happens sometimes, when someone remembers

iam3.text: Are privileged (admin) accounts separated and monitored?
iam3.a2: Separate admin accounts with logging of privileged actions
iam3.a0: The same accounts are used for everything

infra1.text: Are systems and software patched on a defined schedule?

infra2.text: When was your last penetration test?

infra3.text: Are backups automated, protected, and restore-tested?
infra3.a2: Yes, and we have actually tested a restore
infra3.a1: Backups exist but restores are untested

people1.text: Do employees receive security awareness training?

people2.text: Would staff know how to report a suspicious email or incident?
people2.a2: Yes, a clear channel exists and is actually used
people2.a1: Probably not, or only some would

people3.text: Is company data on personal devices (BYOD) controlled?
people3.a2: Managed via policy and technical controls

res1.text: Do you have an incident response plan?
res1.a2: Documented and exercised at least once
res1.a1: A draft exists but was never tested

res2.text: Could you detect a breach in progress?
res2.a2: Monitoring and alerting are in place and reviewed
res2.a1: Some logs exist but nobody watches them

res3.text: Could the business operate through a major outage or ransomware event?
res3.a2: A continuity plan exists and has been tested
res3.a1: A theoretical plan exists on paper

assessment.idSub: Optional: tell us who you are so we can follow up with context. Leave blank for an anonymous score.

gradeDesc.A: Your posture reflects a mature, actively managed security program. The remaining work is sharpening the edge: adversary simulation, continuous validation, and keeping pace with your threat model.
gradeDesc.B: Solid foundations with identifiable gaps. You are ahead of most peers, but a motivated attacker would still find seams. Targeted hardening closes them.
gradeDesc.C: A developing posture: real controls exist, but coverage is uneven and an attacker has meaningful room to operate. Prioritized remediation would change your risk profile quickly.
gradeDesc.D: Your organization is exposed. Several fundamental controls are missing or unmanaged, and a routine attack would likely succeed. This is recoverable, but it needs structured attention now.
gradeDesc.E: Critical exposure. The basics that stop opportunistic attacks are largely absent. The good news: starting from here, the first improvements deliver dramatic risk reduction.

recs.gov: Establish governance: approved policies, clear security ownership, and third-party risk review. Our GRC practice builds this with you, mapped to ISO 27001.
recs.iam: Close identity gaps first: enforce MFA everywhere, review access rights, separate admin accounts. Then validate with a penetration test.
recs.infra: Get patching under SLA, test your restores, and put your infrastructure through an adversary-grade penetration test to find what scanning misses.
recs.people: Your people are your widest attack surface. Deploy behavior-changing training with phishing simulation and a clear incident reporting channel.
recs.resilience: Build detection and response capability: monitoring, an exercised incident response plan, and executive-level security direction. A vCISO accelerates all three.

assessment.closing: This self-assessment is a directional signal, not an audit. For an evidence-based view of your posture, talk to us.
assessment.failed: Could not submit the assessment. Check your connection and retry.
assessment.rateLimited: Too many assessments from this network right now. Try again in a few minutes.

portal.seoDesc: Secure client access to Skelion engagements, findings and remediation tracking.
portal.sub: Track your engagements, review findings as they land, and follow remediation to closure.
portal.rateLimited: Too many attempts. Try again in a few minutes.
portal.noAccount: Access is provisioned by your Skelion engagement lead. No self-registration.
portal.wsSub: Everything below is scoped to your organization. Findings update as our team works.
portal.pwTooShort: New password must be at least 12 characters.
portal.noEngagements: No engagements yet. Your Skelion lead will activate your first engagement here.
portal.noFindings: No findings recorded for this engagement yet.
